title: Chenkostea
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-text-color: #FFF; } }} {{Blurbackground|file=END.png}}
'''''The Moon''''' is beautiful, isn't it? ''Look at the sky and look at '''The Moon''','' ''you will see how precious '''She''' is.'' ''Praise '''The Moon''', adore '''The Moon'''.'' '''''She''' loves us all, '''She''' protects us all.''
'''Embrace The Moon.'''
'''...''' '''Enough of this.''' '''Your control over our world has gone on for far too long''' '''And now''' '''We''' '''Are''' '''Finally''' '''F R E E'''

{{DISPLAYTITLE: '''T̶H̶E̶ ̶M̶O̶O̶N̶''' // Chenkostea}}
{{CSS|content= :root { --theme-page-background-color--secondary:rgba(0,0,0,0.8); --theme-page-background-color:rgba(0,0,0,0.3); .rail-module__header { color: white; } .recent-wiki-activity__username { color: white; } }}
'''Notice''' As per the 1994 Chenkostean Research Agreement, the sharing of any material in this document to unauthorized individuals or groups will result in immediate termination.
{{Class Ψ|color-h=277|color-s=98%|color-l=39%|security= Moderate Spatial Instability|entity= Absence of Sentient Life-Forms|safety= Fatal Psionic Hazards}} ==Description== '''Chenkostea''' is an enigmatic level of the Backrooms consisting of a terrestrial planet sharing many physical characteristics of [[The Frontrooms|Earth]]. The main geological difference between Chenkostea and Earth is the arrangement of Chenkostea's landmasses, which while having the same amount of land area as Earth, are clustered in a singular supercontinent stretching from Chenkostea's North Pole to the equivalent of the southern point of South America. This supercontinent was given no name by the native inhabitants of Chenkostea, who only referred to it as "the land", as there are no other major landmasses on the planet. Proposed names for the supercontinent include Paragaea and Seleneia, but none have been officially adopted. The coastline and northern portions of the supercontinent are mostly forested, hosting a variety of climates ranging from tropical rainforests to arctic taiga. Conversely, the interior of the continent consists mostly of arid land and deserts, and were thus less densely populated. {{Blurimage|file=THE WORLD.png|align=right|hovertext=A shame.|width=250px|caption=Map of Chenkostea's surface}} Chenkostea's geology also seems to be similar to that of Earth, although studies so far have not been in-depth. The level boasts geological features similar to those on Earth, such as canyons, volcanoes, and mountain ranges. Given that most volcanic activity is regulated to small islands in Chenkostea's world-spanning ocean, it is presumed the entirety of the supercontinent lies on a singular tectonic plate. However, despite its ancient-seeming geology, Chenkostea seems to have come into existence far more recently than the Earth, with there being no mention of fossils in recovered scientific texts, and preliminary radiocarbon dating on the oldest known remnants of trees going back to only between 1,200 and 1,300 years ago. This has led to speculation that Chenkostea was created by some force or entity rather than [[System Failure|being an alternate timeline]] that merged into the Backrooms, like [[Level 398|certain other Earth-like levels]]. Chenkostea boasts a level of biodiversity rare in the Backrooms, being nearly the same as that on Earth. However, the level notably has a complete lack of animal life, being populated by only plants, fungi, and microorganisms. These lifeforms inhabit roughly the same climates as on Earth and coexist with other species in ecosystems equivalent to them. However, due to the absence of animal life, the biosphere and carbon cycle on Chenkostea became unbalanced, and caused a mass extinction as populations spiraled out of control and then plummeted, with populations only beginning to stabilize in some portions of the planet. However, remains of animals, in addition to analysis of the ruins of former human civilization on Chenkostea has led to the belief that some form of apocalyptic event, likely the first instance of the Lunar Flare phenomenon, occurred about 100 years ago, and led to the deaths of all organisms with any cognitive capacity. Despite these similarities to The Frontrooms, Chenkostea has begun to take on attributes of the Backrooms since the event. The level's spatial structure has become unstable, leading to geometry, especially in human-made structures, to twist and distort. This results in phenomena such as certain buildings having their interiors be larger than their exteriors, and the appearance of spatial rifts leading to the [[The Broken]] or even [[Uncharted Territory]]. It is thought that at the current rate of decay, the level will fully collapse around 600 years from now, after which it would fall into The Broken. Theoretically, these spatial disruptions should produce the ideal conditions for [[Phenomenon 10|Spawning]] to occur. However, no entities have been found within the level, potentially due to a rapid termination of Spawned entities during the Lunar Flare event. === Former Human Habitation === The main factor that sets Chenkostea apart from other levels of The Backrooms is the presence of former human civilization in the level. Ruins of man-made structures, including entire cities and nations, have been found across the supercontinent and on the islands. The majority of archaeological work has been done on towns and cities within the inner desert of the supercontinent, as the lack of precipitation and extreme weather has helped to preserve the structures. Additionally, a multitude of written documents in various initially unknown languages have been discovered. Based on archaeological evidence, the civilization was technologically equivalent or even superior to humanity in the present-day Frontrooms, having mastered computing, powered flight, modern medicine, and satellite communication, as well as some advances not yet achieved by humanity on Earth, such as fusion power generation, advanced composite materials serving as stronger versions of concrete, and biological immortality (although this only seemed to be available to the very rich). The inhabitants of Chenkostea had no knowledge of the existence of the Backrooms, with its reality seeming to once have been more stable than even that of the Frontrooms, preventing any means of noclipping. Additionally, Chenkosteans had nearly no knowledge of anomalous or supernatural phenomena beyond the lunar phenomena revered by their religion. However, there is one notable exception to this; the knowledge of [[Object 499|Kharthic Energy]]. Recovered documents from multiple nations describe devices able to harvest ambient Khartic Energy from the environment. The extracted energy was mainly used as recreational drugs, although there is evidence it also had applications in warfare. The timekeeping system used in Chenkostea was identical to that which is near-universally used on Earth in the modern day, having 24-hour days and 7-day weeks, with the exception of the existence of a lunar calendar rather than a solar calendar, with an year of 354 days consisting of twelve 29 to 30 day lunations. The Chenkosteans claimed that their calendar began at the creation of their world, and designated their years as AL (''Anno Lunae''). As the year recorded at the time of the apocalyptic event was 1266, matching radiocarbon dating, this seems to have some basis in truth. As estimates place the destructive event as occurring roughly 140 to 150 Gregorian years ago, making the current year on the Chenkostean calendar between 1410 and 1420 AL. Through linguistic and genetic analasis, it was shown that most populations on Chenkostea are descended from various ethnocultural groups derived from Earth, although there are also some unconfirmed links to ancient civilizations that originated within the Backroom. Notably though, the climates where these peoples settled as well as their cultures and systems of government vary greatly from their counterparts in The Frontrooms. The only non-chenkostean language known to be used is the Latin language, which appears to have been used in religious contexts worldwide.. Politically, Chenkostea was split into 10 nations. Almost all of these countries had advanced to developed living standards, and in total had a population of around 4 billion people. At the time of the mass extinction event, the world was in a state of relative peace, with no wars having broken out in the last 50 years before the extinction, with the last war being an invasion of the totalitarian state of Donimajapa by an international coalition. Despite lasting tensions caused by differing political systems, this peace for worldwide trade and prosperity, dominated by the spheres of influence of three superpowers: * Capicamarca, a highly advanced nation. Capicamarca's political system was a constitutional monarchy combined with a hybrid of technocracy and democracy. This "techno-democratic" system which entrusts decision-making to both an elected Parliament and technocratic commissions is the most pervasive in modern Chenkostea, having prevailed in their equivalent of the Cold War, which lasted from 1154 to 1191 AL. Capicamarca's main language, called Capicamarcan, seems to have developed from Vulgar Latin, and their population is mainly descended from the people of of Southern Europe. * The Federation of Noretlan, a federation composed after the unification of four nations in 1083 AL, also having a techno-democratic system. Noretlan was composed of populations descended from indigenous Americans, Middle Easterners, and West Africans, with the largest chunk of their population being Mesoamerican in origin, and their primary language being derived from Uto-Aztecan language family. Before "recent" reforms, the government of Noretlan was an anocracy, with their head of state having significant power over the legislature. * Kheraganuur, the largest nation by former population and land area, encompassing a large portion of the central desert and southwestern plains. The nation was composed primarily of populations descended from various Central Asian populations, and its language contains Persian and Turkic roots. During the Chenkostean Cold War Kheraganuur was an ultranationalist totalitarian dictatorship. After a coup in 1199 AL, a new government took power. While still a dictatorship, the new Kheraganuur government heavily relaxed surveillance of citizens and xenophobic rhetoric, normalizing relations with other nations. === The Moon // '''Mater Lunae '''=== One of Chenkostea's most defining features is its natural satellite, which while superficially being a copy of Earth's moon, possess multiple unique properties and had great significance in Chenkostean culture and religion. Chenkostea's moon is surrounded by incredibly large amounts of Khartic Energy, only rivalled by that of the physical manifestations of the Achospic Plane itself that occasionaly appear within The Void. This veil of Khartic Energy has the effect of making the moon's appearance shifting and inconstant, taking forms ranging from a cartoonish cratered sphere to a churning ball covered in multicolored television static. These altered appearances rapidly fluctuate and seem to be linked to observation, as the moon seems to change form more rapidly while being observed, and has even been seen differently by multiple people observing simultaneously. If one gazes at the moon for a prolonged periods of time, they will begin to experience multiple mental effect, a phenomenon known as Selenoplexia, named after an obsolete medical condition. Initially, wanderers report experiencing auditory hallucinations of illegible whispering and blurring at the edge of one's vision. After around half a minute of staring at the moon, the level of hallucination will increase, advancing into visual hallucinations, typically beginning with random patches of shape and color, and then transitioning to more defined forms, which greatly vary in appearance but commonly relate to the moon and/or religious symbolism. After several minutes, the person undergoing Selenoplexia will become unresponsive, as if put in a trance, and will remain in this state unless removed from the level. {{Blurimage|file=MOTHER.jpeg|align=right|hovertext=|width=250px|caption=Image of a '''Lunar Flare''' event.}} The main danger on Chenkostea, and why the main impediment to settlement and long-term exploration of the level is the Lunar Flare event, which occurs every 15 days. Minutes before the beginning of the event, the sky darkens, and a full moon becomes visible in the sky, regardless of the geographical location of the observer or current lunar phase. Shortly after, the "moon" in the sky will glow vivid blue, illuminating everything around it as well as causing heavy spatial distortions around it. The moon will then emit a fatal burst of psionic energy, instantly killing any organism or entity with any form of brain anywhere in the level. No form of psychic shielding or use of substances such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] has been known to be effective at countering this effect. Thus, it is thought that a Lunar Flare event is what caused the extinction of animal life on Chenkostea. Interestingly, a variant of the Lunar Flare event had occurred on Chenkostea before the extinction. This version of the event was non-lethal, with it instead being described as giving onlookers "divine euphoria". Chenkostea's moon was central in the religion of the Chenkostean people, being personified as a deity known as Mater Lunae, typically depicted as a human-like woman with gray hair and pale, ashen skin adorned in white clothing. Lunarism stated that Mater Lunae created Chenkostea, its sun, and its moon, and that nothing else existed within the universe, likely due to to the lack of stars within the level. It also contained doctrines on basic moral rules and societal norms, in addition to a prohibition on disrespecting the moon or related symbols, but did not have any dietary restrictions or set days of worship. While originally assumed to be simply a mythological figure, revovered video and audio records show that she did in fact exist, and semi-frequently made visits to the planet. Due to the heavy presnse of Khartic Energy on the moon, it is widely believed this is an instance of [[Phenomenon 9|Tulpaefication]]. However, as Chenkostean literature did not mention any other occurrences of Tulpaefictation in the level, despite their culture containing many legendary figures, it is brought into question whether Tulpas can manifest within the level. This has led some to believe that she was instead an entity originating elsewhere, potentially from the Backrooms, which entered Chenkostea. Mater Lunae was recorded to have access to many supernatural powers, including "instantaneous travel to any one place, the making and unmaking of objects, and control over the material world" as well as alleged Omnipotence and omniscience, although the latter two were likely exaggerations or fabricated by religious authorities. According to Lunarist theological doctrine, Mater Lunae simultaneously embodied both her humanoid form and the moon itself, her true form being a immaterial essence inhabiting both. While a complete record of appearances by Mater Lunae is not known, as many occurred in isolated areas until the advent of modern technology on Chenkostea, they were known to be semi-frequent, with dozens occurring per year. According to available records, these visits typically consisted of Mater Lunae manifesting near the location of a seemingly random person or group, and attempting to make conversation with the understandably startled person, before either relaying a message for the people of Chenkostea or inviting the individual to visit her home. The nature of this "home" is unknown, potentially existing in another level, with those recorded as having visited it only having vague memories of it, describing it as "beautiful". Notably, some who were taken to Mater Lunae's home did not return. This fact was seemingly did not raise concern among Chenkosteans, who wrote of it offhandedly. ==History of Exploration== While a definitive date of discovery is unknown, the earliest confirmed entry to Chenkostea was in 1979, although that report, contains evidence for earlier exploration of the level. Chenkostea became known to the [[M.E.G.]] 6 years later in 1985, when a M.E.G exploration team in [[Level 696]], utilized [[Phenomenon 1-A]] to skip forward to [[Level 700]]. Overwhelmed by the level's hostile environment, they attempted to take shelter in a tunnel, and inadvertently entered Chenkostea, discovering the remains of a city days later. After determining that the city was human-made and not naturally generated by the Backrooms, the team sent 2 members to exit the level and contact the rest of the group through [[Phenomenon 2|BACK_NET]]. Upon identifying a consistent entrance in [[Level 700]] and entering it nearly a month later, it was discovered that the entirety of the original team had perished from the Lunar Flare event. After several failed expeditions, the nature of the Lunar Flare event was determined, and to accomidate the 15-day cycle of the event, a base was established outside the ruins of a major city in Capiamarca, connected to the M.E.G's central base in [[The Hub]] by an [[Object 55|Interlevel Hyperlink]], allowing rapid evacuation and re-entry during a Lunar Flare, as well as easy rotation of personnel and removal of artifacts from the level. Meanwhile, archaeology in the Backrooms had begun to be a popular scientific topic in the 1990s following the discoveries of ancient human Habitation in [[Level 8]], [[Level 46]], and [[Level 370]]. Due to this and the increased demand for personnel to help research Chenkostea, the M.E.G's council of Impresarios approved a proposal by Impresarios Elizabeth Kaufmann and Heinz Bernadotte establishing the M.E.G. Archaeostudies Division. The next major development in the exploration of Chenkostea occurred in 1994, when members of the [[Backrooms Colonists]] in [[Level 246]] accidentally found an entrance within an underwater cave leading to Chenkostea. As the entrance led to a beach off the coast of a major city once belonging to the nation of Semmundigen, leading to them discovering the nature of the level much more rapidly. Upon exiting the level through a spatial rift within the city, the Colonists reached out to the M.E.G and the [[Phenomenon 57|Wake Historical Society]] to aid in exploring the level, not knowing that the former already had knowledge of it. Due to the M.E.G and the Colonists having good relations in the time period, the two groups quickly formulated and signed the 1994 Chenkostean Research Agreement, the two groups agreeing to mutual aid in exploration and a maintaining secrecy regarding the level. An year later, the two groups were able to pressure the Wake Historical Society into accepting the agreement. While tensions have emerged between the Colonists and the M.E.G following the former's support of the [[Republic of Aurielle]] during the Korthie Casiagen War in the 2000s, the deal has held to the present day. == Recovered Texts ==
Growing up in Mexico, I was fascinated by the history of my country and its people. What interested me most was the ancient Mesoamerican civilizations that ruled my country before the Spanish conquest. When I was 12 or 13, after much pleading to my parents, they drove me to see one of their old cities all the way in Yucatán. As I walked through the weather-worn structures, I thought of the fate that befell their builders, overtaken by brutality and disease, and how their descendants still live in Mexico and shaped the country's culture and history. These thoughts churning fresh in my mind, I went on to study archaology in university, and was actually at a site when I fell out of the world and into The Backrooms. You've probably heard similar stories to mine ad nauseum, but to put it simply, I fell into [[Level 1]], got picked up by a wandering group, and travelled through [[The Hub]] to [[Level 154]]. I stayed in Level 154 with the nameless group for another few years, until I heard that the Wake Historical Society was recruiting archaeologists. As I felt I was not acomplishing much in Level 154, I bade farewell to my newfound friends, and took the train to their headquarters in [[Level 10]]. Expecting to be put to work looking at old bones in [[Level 8]], I was shown something beyond my wildest dreams: Chenkostea. It would be reasonable to say I was quickly overwhelmed by the sheer scope of what we were seeing. An entire planet, as rich in culture and history as Earth is. Luckily, my knowledge of Mesoamerican languages was invaluable in decoding some of the new languages descended from them. In a way, although their culture and social structures were almost completely different, I was still learning about the peoples I had been so curious about in my youth. The longer I study this level, the more that nagging question fills my mind: What happened here? While emptiness and ruin are common within the Backrooms, instead of giving the feeling of unearthly desolation, Chenkostea simply feels like a tomb, even more so than [[Level 207|a level entirely made of tombs]]. What could have caused so much death and destruction, the erasure of billions overnight, no culture, no legacy, simply ruins. And their loving protector too is nowhere to be found, only a wrathful moon. Could the empathy in their collective unconscious have turned to spite, their psionic energy making a moon that gave off warmth and kindness into a destroyer of worlds? Or could something have snapped in Mater Lunae's mind, causing her to rain fire on her people. I know for a fact an answer is somewhere in there, under the rubble of a dead world, waiting for us to find it. -Ramona Garcia, Wake Historical Society archaeologist
{{Collapse|num=3|initial=
'''Warning: An unauthorized edit has been made to this document. Open?
'''|body= The Wake. Irreality. Asphodel. Limbo. The In-Between. Limspace. The Backrooms. She called it the only lie she ever told, although that too was a lie. She saved them from that terrible realm, gave them shelter, a new home, bathed them in her love every half-moon. She even abandoned her old realm, promising one day to return. All for a greater cause. To stop destruction. To save this wretched reality. Even if it took a thousand years. But mankind is not content with Paradise. If you give man a closed door, they will try to open it at whatever cost, and hiding from Mother's watchful eye, they tore a hole in the world, and saw the truth that lay beyond. When they returned, they brought with them the fire of infinity, and lashed out at the one who had shown them nothing but love. And the world was covered in moonlight. |final= Close?}} ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== * Entering a certain cave in [[Level 700]] or [[Level 246]] * Several static [[Object 55|Hyperlinks]] have been created to Chenkostea in various levels ===Exits=== * Entering spatial rifts brings one to a seemingly random level
